div.head: The Acting Secretary of State to the American Member of the Inter-American Neutrality Committee (Fenwick)
div.p: My Dear Professor Fenwick: With reference to your letter of July 2, it is my hope that I will, in the relatively near future, have an opportunity to discuss with you the various interesting points which you raise with regard to the work of the Inter-American Neutrality Committee.

It is my opinion that your thought that the Committee at some future time might be charged by the American republics with a longrange study of the problem of peace from the point of view of the Western Hemisphere deserves very serious consideration. I am inclined, at the same time, to agree with you that this question would probably not come within the scope of the current Meeting at Habana.
